seoTitle: Parklea vs Parkville — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Parklea and Parkville. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Parklea and Parkville.
heading: Parklea vs Parkville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Parklea, New South Wales and Parkville, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Parklea has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Parkville (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Parklea or Parkville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Parklea has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Parkville scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Parklea scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. Parkville scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Parklea or Parkville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Parklea has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
